docs.summary: Configuration file for the takeitgo-lite-backend Node.js project. Defines project metadata, scripts for development and production, and lists project dependencies.

config_variables.name: PORT
config_variables.description: The port number the application will listen on. Defaults to 3000.

config_variables.name: MONGO_URI
config_variables.description: The connection URI for the MongoDB database.

config_variables.name: JWT_SECRET
config_variables.description: The secret key used for signing JSON Web Tokens (JWTs).

config_variables.name: JWT_EXPIRES_IN
config_variables.description: The expiration time for JWTs. This is a time duration string (e.g., '1h', '7d').

config_variables.name: GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY
config_variables.description: Optional: API key for Google Maps integration. Uncomment and provide your key if needed.

docs.summary: Main server file for the Take iT & Go Lite API. Sets up Express, Socket.IO, connects to MongoDB, and defines middleware and routes. Includes error handling and graceful shutdown.

config.description: Loads environment variables from a .env file.
Creates an HTTP server using the Express app.
Initializes Socket.IO server with CORS configuration.

routes.summary: Health check endpoint for the API.

200.message: Take iT & Go Lite API is running!

routes.summary: Mounts all API routes defined in ./src/routes.
routes.description: All subsequent API requests will be routed through this path.

sockets.description: Initializes and registers the chat socket handler.

eventHandlers.description: Logs unhandled promise rejections and gracefully shuts down the server.
Logs uncaught exceptions and gracefully shuts down the server.

docs.summary: Establishes a connection to the MongoDB database using Mongoose.

Configuration for the application logger using Winston. This utility sets up logging levels, formats, and transports (files and console for development). It ensures errors are logged with stack traces and messages are formatted for readability and JSON compatibility.

Custom error class for API-related errors, extending Node.js's built-in Error class. Includes a statusCode property to denote HTTP status codes and an isOperational flag to distinguish from programming errors.
Utility functions for password hashing and comparison using bcrypt.
Utility functions for JWT (JSON Web Token) generation and verification.
A middleware function that logs incoming HTTP requests.
Custom error handling middleware to process and format API errors.

utility.description: This middleware intercepts errors thrown within the application, standardizes them into an 'ApiError' format, and sends a consistent JSON response to the client. It specifically handles common errors like Mongoose CastErrors, duplicate keys, validation errors, and Joi validation errors, translating them into user-friendly messages and appropriate HTTP status codes. For unhandled errors, it defaults to a 500 Internal Server Error.
details: Handles Mongoose 'CastError' (e.g., invalid ObjectId format) with a 404 status.
Handles Mongoose duplicate key errors (code 11000) with a 400 status.
Handles Mongoose 'ValidationError' with a 400 status, aggregating validation messages.
Handles Joi validation errors ('isJoi' property) with a 400 status, aggregating detail messages.
Logs all errors to the configured logger for debugging purposes.
Forwards unhandled errors with a default 500 status code.

docs.summary: Provides middleware functions for authentication and authorization.

middleware.name: protect
middleware.summary: Protects routes by verifying JWT tokens and attaching user information to the request object.
middleware.description: This middleware checks for a JWT token in the 'Authorization' header (Bearer schema). If a valid token is found, it verifies the token, retrieves the associated user from the database, and attaches the user object (excluding the password) to `req.user`. If the token is missing, invalid, or the user is not found, it returns an appropriate `ApiError`.

middleware.name: authorize
middleware.summary: Authorizes routes based on user roles.
middleware.description: This middleware takes a variable number of roles as arguments. It checks if the authenticated user (`req.user`) has one of the specified roles. If not, it returns a `403 Forbidden` error.

parameters.name: roles
parameters.description: An array of allowed user roles.

docs.summary: Middleware for validating request bodies against Joi schemas.

utility.name: validate
utility.description: Creates a validation middleware for a given Joi schema. It validates the request body, updates it with the validated and stripped value, and passes it to the next middleware or route handler. If validation fails, it passes the Joi error to the error handler.

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: The Joi schema to validate the request body against.

returns.description: A middleware function with signature (req, res, next).

validation.message: Please add a valid phone number
Password must be at least 6 characters

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the user was created

hooks.description: Hashes the password before saving the user document to the database.

status.description: The current status of the payment.

transactionId.description: Unique identifier for the transaction from the payment service. Allows null values.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the payment record was created.

deliveryId.description: Reference to the Delivery document this chat belongs to.

senderId.description: Reference to the User document of the sender.

receiverId.description: Reference to the User document of the receiver.

message.description: The content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the message was created.

read.description: Indicates whether the message has been read by the receiver.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of the GPS log entry

email.description: User's email address. Must be a valid email format.

phone.description: User's phone number. Must be in a valid format.

password.description: User's password. Must be at least 6 characters long.

role.description: User's role, either 'shipper' or 'traveler'. Defaults to 'shipper'.

messages.string.email: Email must be a valid email address
messages.string.pattern.base: Phone number must be a valid format
messages.string.min: Password must be at least 6 characters long

email.description: User's email address to initiate password reset.

newPassword.description: The new password for the user. Must be at least 6 characters long.

utility.summary: Provides authentication-related functionalities including user signup, login, and password reset.

functions.name: signup
functions.description: Registers a new user with the provided credentials.

parameters.name: userData
parameters.description: An object containing user registration data.

role.description: The role assigned to the user (e.g., 'user', 'admin').

returns.description: An object containing the newly created user's basic information and an authentication token.

id.description: The unique identifier of the user.

role.description: The role assigned to the user.

token.description: A JSON Web Token (JWT) for user authentication.

throws.description: Thrown if a user with the provided email or phone number already exists.

functions.name: login
functions.description: Authenticates a user based on their email and password.

returns.description: An object containing the authenticated user's basic information and an authentication token.

id.description: The unique identifier of the user.

role.description: The role assigned to the user.

token.description: A JSON Web Token (JWT) for user authentication.

throws.description: Thrown if the provided credentials are invalid (user not found or password mismatch).

functions.name: resetPassword
functions.description: Resets the password for a user identified by their email.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address of the user whose password needs to be reset.

parameters.name: newPassword
parameters.description: The new password for the user.

returns.description: A success message indicating the password has been reset.

throws.description: Thrown if no user is found with the provided email address.

utility.name: matchService
utility.summary: Provides functionality to find matching deliveries for travelers or travelers for deliveries.

functions.name: findMatches
functions.summary: Finds suitable matches for a given delivery request based on user role. Currently returns mock data.

parameters.name: deliveryId
parameters.description: The ID of the delivery request to find matches for.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The ID of the user initiating the match request.

parameters.name: userRole
parameters.description: The role of the user ('shipper' or 'traveler') to determine matching logic.

delivery.description: The details of the delivery request.

_id.description: The unique identifier for the delivery.

itemDescription.description: Description of the item to be delivered.

pickupDate.description: The desired pickup date and time.

deliveryDate.description: The desired delivery date and time.

id.description: The ID of the matched entity (e.g., traveler ID).

name.description: The name of the matched entity.

rating.description: The rating of the matched entity.

pickupTime.description: Estimated pickup time for the match.

dropoffTime.description: Estimated drop-off time for the match.

message.description: A message indicating the current status of the matching logic.

functions.notes: This function is a placeholder. The actual implementation will involve complex matching algorithms, potentially including geographic proximity, time availability, and item compatibility. It may also integrate with external APIs like Google Maps.

utility.summary: Provides services for tracking delivery GPS locations and status updates.

functions.name: startTracking
functions.summary: Initiates tracking for a delivery, setting its status to 'in-transit' and recording the initial GPS location.

params.name: deliveryId
params.description: The ID of the delivery to start tracking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user initiating the tracking (typically the traveler).

params.name: lat
params.description: The initial latitude of the delivery.

params.name: lng
params.description: The initial longitude of the delivery.

message.description: A success message indicating tracking has started.

deliveryId.description: The ID of the delivery being tracked.

errors.description: Cannot start tracking a completed or cancelled delivery.

functions.name: stopTracking
functions.summary: Stops tracking for a delivery and marks its status as 'delivered'.

params.name: deliveryId
params.description: The ID of the delivery to stop tracking.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user initiating the stop tracking (typically the traveler).

message.description: A success message indicating tracking has stopped and the delivery is marked as delivered.

errors.description: Delivery is not currently in transit.

functions.name: getTracking
functions.summary: Retrieves the tracking history (GPS logs) for a specific delivery.

params.name: deliveryId
params.description: The ID of the delivery to retrieve tracking data for.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user requesting the tracking data.

params.name: userRole
params.description: The role of the user requesting the tracking data (e.g., 'shipper', 'traveler').

gpsLogs.description: An array of GPS log objects for the delivery, sorted by timestamp.

message.description: A success message indicating tracking data was retrieved.

docs.summary: Provides functions to manage payment escrows and fund releases for deliveries.

utility.description: Initiates a mock escrow for a delivery, holding funds until release.  Requires the shipper to create it for a 'pending' or 'matched' delivery.

params.name: deliveryId
params.description: The ID of the delivery to create an escrow for.

params.name: amount
params.description: The amount to be held in escrow.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user initiating the escrow (must be the shipper).

params.name: userRole
params.description: The role of the user initiating the escrow (not strictly enforced in mock).

errors.message: Only the shipper can create escrow for this delivery
Escrow cannot be created for a delivery in this state
Escrow already exists and is pending for this delivery

utility.description: Releases funds from escrow for a delivered package. Requires the shipper to trigger it and the delivery to be in a 'delivered' state.

params.name: deliveryId
params.description: The ID of the delivery for which to release funds.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user initiating the fund release (must be the shipper).

params.name: userRole
params.description: The role of the user initiating the fund release (not strictly enforced in mock).

errors.message: Only the shipper can release funds for this delivery
No pending escrow found for this delivery
Funds can only be released for a delivered package. Current status: [status]

docs.summary: Handles WebSocket connections for real-time chat functionality within specific delivery contexts. It manages user authentication via JWT, room joining based on delivery ID, fetching recent messages, and broadcasting new messages.

sockets.summary: Establishes a new WebSocket connection, performs authentication and authorization for chat access based on a JWT token and delivery ID, joins the user to a delivery-specific chat room, and loads recent messages.

authentication.validation: Verifies JWT to obtain userId and userRole.

deliveryId.validation: Checks for presence of deliveryId and verifies if the authenticated user (shipper or traveler) is authorized to chat for that delivery.

response_on_connection.description: Emits a list of the 50 most recent chat messages for the delivery upon successful connection and authentication.
Emits an error message if authentication or setup fails.

listeners.summary: Handles incoming chat messages from a connected client. It validates the message, stores it in the database, and broadcasts it to all users in the relevant delivery chat room.

response_on_event.description: Broadcasts the newly sent message to all clients in the delivery chat room.
Emits an error message if there's an issue sending the message.

listeners.summary: Logs when a socket connection is terminated.
Logs errors occurring on the socket connection.

docs.summary: Node.js backend for the 'Take iT & Go' peer-to-peer delivery platform, featuring user authentication, route matching, mock payments, GPS tracking, and real-time chat.

routes.summary: Register a new user
Log in an existing user
Reset user's password
Find potential matches for a delivery (Protected)
Start tracking for a delivery (Protected)
Stop tracking and mark a delivery as delivered (Protected)
Get tracking history for a delivery (Protected)
Create a mock escrow payment (Protected, Shipper role)
Release mock escrow funds (Protected, Shipper role)

sockets.authentication: JWT token via socket.handshake.auth.token or socket.handshake.query.token, deliveryId via socket.handshake.query.deliveryId

newMessage.summary: Server broadcasts a new message to the chat room

events.recentMessages: Server sends recent messages upon successful connection to a delivery chat.
events.authError: Server sends error messages related to authentication.
events.chatError: Server sends error messages related to chat operations.
